Tests if the modulus of negative infinity and 3 is NaN.

# Purpose
This code is a simple assertion test that checks the result of the modulus operation between negative infinity and the number 3. The expression `-Infinity % 3` is expected to return `NaN` (Not-a-Number), and the `assert` function verifies this condition. If the result is not `NaN`, the assertion will fail, indicating an error in the expected behavior of the modulus operation with negative infinity. This code provides narrow functionality, focusing on a specific mathematical operation and its expected outcome.
